runTitle: Local dev turbopack trace - dashboard lazy shell/server section follow-up

completedImprovements.title: Split DashboardClient into auth wrapper and lazy interactive shell
completedImprovements.note: DashboardClient now keeps auth/hydration gating in the entry chunk and loads DashboardInteractiveShell with client-only dynamic import.

completedImprovements.title: Lazy load ServerDashboard from DashboardContent
completedImprovements.note: ServerDashboard moved behind a client-only dynamic boundary so dashboard summary/system overview can render before the heavy card and modal graph loads.

pendingImprovements.title: Trace instrumentation/layout/provider compile bottleneck
pendingImprovements.note: After the lazy shell and ServerDashboard section split, /dashboard cold trace is still 133s total (http 200), so the main dev bottleneck remains unresolved outside the moved dashboard subtree.

notes: Type-check, lint, test:quick, and targeted vitest suites all passed after the dashboard lazy-loading refactor.
The follow-up turbopack trace finished in 133s total with trace file size about 171.8MB, effectively unchanged from the prior 134s run.
Current evidence suggests the remaining dev cold-start bottleneck is not materially reduced by moving DashboardClient and ServerDashboard behind client-only dynamic boundaries.
